concerns.text: Task T016a explicitly instructs to 'DO NOT filter out rows with 0 tokens' and preserve ALL rows. This directly contradicts FR-011 in spec.md which mandates: 'The system must filter out rows with null energy or 0 tokens during aggregation.' This is a semantic weakening of a functional requirement; the task deliverable (aggregated CSV) will fail to satisfy the spec's constraint if implemented as written.

Task T036 mandates raising a DataFetchError and exiting with code 1 if real data fetch fails, with no fallback or skip mechanism. This violates Constitution Principle I (Reproducibility) which requires results to be reproducible on a fresh runner. If the external HumanEval dataset is temporarily unreachable, the pipeline fails permanently rather than handling the transient error, breaking the 'fresh runner' guarantee without a spec-defined skip or retry strategy.

Task T009 calculates the checksum of data/raw/human_eval_data.jsonl but states: 'If file missing, mark as pending (do not fail).' This violates Constitution Principle III (Data Hygiene) which requires datasets to be checksummed and recorded before proceeding. Allowing a 'pending' state for a missing raw data file permits the pipeline to proceed without verified data integrity, creating a silent failure mode for data hygiene.

Task T027 defines 'Energy per Correct Solution' as Total Energy / Count of Passed Problems and instructs to set the value to 'null' and exclude the point if Count is 0. This violates FR-006 (Visualization) which requires a scatter plot of 'Energy/Correct vs Accuracy'. If a model has 0% accuracy, excluding it entirely from the plot obscures the comparison for the least efficient model, failing to satisfy the requirement to present the trade-off for all tested models.
